testcase.name: test_live_chat_multiline_labeled_prompt

failure.message:
AssertionError: citation[0].section null — grouping failed. c0={'act': 'Income Tax Act, 1961', 'section': None, 'note': None}, all cits=[{'act': 'Income Tax Act, 1961', 'section': None, 'note': None}], content_tail=" a tax audit

2. **Once you opt out** (by declaring lower profit or otherwise), you **cannot use 44AD for the next 5 assessment years**.

3. **Advance tax:** Pay advance tax on the deemed income. No presumption relief here.

4. **Depreciation:** Allowed as per IT Act rates, but only if you declare profit higher than the presumptive rate.

## Why Students Get This Wrong

- Confusing 44AD (business) with 44ADA (professionals).
- Forgetting the 5-year bar after opting out.
- Assuming expenses are separately deductible (they're NOT — the 6%/8% already factors in all expenses).

---

**SOURCES:**

Act/Standard: Income Tax Act, 1961

Section/Para: Section 44AD

Note: Presumptive taxation scheme for small businesses with turnover up to ₹2 crore; deemed profit of 6% (cash) or 8% (digital receipts)" assert None + where None = <built-in method get of dict object at 0xfa6205f81dc0>('section') + where <built-in method get of dict object at 0xfa6205f81dc0> = {'act': 'Income Tax Act, 1961', 'section': None, 'note': None}.get

testcase.failure:
backend/tests/test_phase3_fixes.py:294: in test_live_chat_multiline_labeled_prompt assert c0.get("section"), \ E AssertionError: citation[0].section null — grouping failed. c0={'act': 'Income Tax Act, 1961', 'section': None, 'note': None}, all cits=[{'act': 'Income Tax Act, 1961', 'section': None, 'note': None}], content_tail=" a tax audit

2. **Once you opt out** (by declaring lower profit or otherwise), you **cannot use 44AD for the next 5 assessment years**.

3. **Advance tax:** Pay advance tax on the deemed income. No presumption relief here.

4. **Depreciation:** Allowed as per IT Act rates, but only if you declare profit higher than the presumptive rate.

## Why Students Get This Wrong

- Confusing 44AD (business) with 44ADA (professionals).
- Forgetting the 5-year bar after opting out.
- Assuming expenses are separately deductible (they're NOT — the 6%/8% already factors in all expenses).

---

**SOURCES:**

Act/Standard: Income Tax Act, 1961

Section/Para: Section 44AD

Note: Presumptive taxation scheme for small businesses with turnover up to ₹2 crore; deemed profit of 6% (cash) or 8% (digital receipts)" E assert None E + where None = <built-in method get of dict object at 0xfa6205f81dc0>('section') E + where <built-in method get of dict object at 0xfa6205f81dc0> = {'act': 'Income Tax Act, 1961', 'section': None, 'note': None}.get
